module.title: ABC Electronics India - SAP TM End-to-End Implementation

learningObjectives.title: Plan transportation with constraints
learningObjectives.description: Create an optimized transportation proposal while balancing SLA, availability and cost.

learningObjectives.title: Understand carrier selection logic
learningObjectives.description: Evaluate carriers using business rule priorities in SAP TM.

learningObjectives.title: Explain optimization outcomes
learningObjectives.description: Justify planning decisions using objective constraints, not price alone.

learningObjectives.title: Validate proposal before approval
learningObjectives.description: Review profiles, resource checks, deadlines and ranking rules before confirming selection.

sections.title: Real-Time Lab 2: Transportation Planning & Carrier Selection

sections.content: The Freight Unit has now been successfully created after resolving the master data issue in Lab 1. The transportation planner must now create an optimized transportation plan while meeting customer delivery commitments and minimizing logistics costs.

sections.title: Your Role
sections.content: You are the Transportation Planner responsible for selecting the most suitable carrier while balancing cost, delivery commitment and resource availability.
The customer requires delivery within 24 hours. SAP TM begins transportation planning and evaluates available carriers based on predefined selection strategies.

Although DHL offers the best price-performance combination, no truck is available. TCI is cheaper than Blue Dart but cannot meet the customer's required delivery time. Blue Dart is more expensive but satisfies both availability and delivery requirements.
The Sales Manager questions why SAP selected Blue Dart instead of DHL, even though DHL is cheaper. He believes the planning engine made an incorrect decision and requests an explanation.

sections.title: Consultant Analysis
rows: Only carrier meeting both availability and delivery commitment.
Did SAP choose the cheapest carrier?
No. SAP chose the optimal carrier based on business rules.

sections.title: Consultant Resolution
sections.content: Explain to the Sales Manager that SAP TM follows optimization rules rather than selecting the lowest-cost carrier. Since DHL lacked an available truck and TCI could not meet the SLA, Blue Dart became the optimal transportation solution despite its higher cost.

sections.title: Project Learning
sections.content: Transportation optimization always considers multiple constraints simultaneously. Cost is only one factor; service level agreements, resource availability and transit times are equally important.

sections.title: Accenture Scenario Question
sections.content: A customer asks why SAP TM selected a more expensive carrier. Explain the decision. The correct approach is to discuss optimization objectives, carrier selection profiles, resource availability and SLA compliance-not simply transportation cost.

items: Project continues to Lab 3: Freight Order Creation & Load Consolidation.
